title: Mandrel and method for manufacturing substantially cylindrical shaped objects
abstract: A mandrel for manufacturing substantially cylindrically shaped objects, in particular storage tanks made of plastic, more particularly of fibre or fibre glass reinforced plastic, wherein the mandrel (M) is configured such that a diameter (D) of a substantially cylindrical outer surface (S) of the mandrel is adjustable between at least a first (D1) and a second diameter (D2), wherein said substantially cylindrical outer surface (S) is uninterrupted for each of the at least first and second diameter. The invention also provides a method for manufacturing substantially cylindrically shaped objects, in particular storage tanks made of plastic, more particularly of fibre or fibre glass reinforced plastic, wherein an at least one object forming substance (11) is applied onto a substantially cylindrical external surface (S) of a mandrel (M).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Mandrel for manufacturing substantially cylindrically shaped objects, in particular storage tanks made of plastic, more particularly of fibre or fibre glass reinforced plastic, characterised in that the mandrel (M) is configured such that a diameter (D) of a substantially cyhndrical outer surface (S) of the mandrel is adjustable between at least a first (D 1) and a second diameter (D2), wherein said substantially cylindrical outer surface (S) is uninterrupted for each of the at least first and second diameter. 2. Mandrel according to claim 1, wherein the outer surface (S) of the mandrel is provided by wall sections (4), which in a circumferential direction slidably overlap each other, wherein overlapping parts of the wall sections are preferably radially biased onto each other. 3. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, including alternatingly positioned first (5) and second wall sections (6) that define said outer surface (S), radially inner sides of the first wall sections (5) facing radially outer sides of the second wall sections (6). 4. Mandrel according to claim 3, wherein said first (5) and/or second (6) wall sections have a varying radial thickness, particularly a thickness which decreases viewed in a circumferential direction from a wall section's middle towards side ends of the respective wall section. 5. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein an outer wall of the mandrel, which outer wall defines said outer surface (S), is composed of a plurality of flexible and resilient wall sections (4-6), for example plates or plate-like elements. 6. Mandrel according to claim 5, wherein said wall sections (4-6) are substantially made of plastic or a composite material, and preferably of a resilient material. 7. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims 5-6, wherein at least one of the wall sections (4-6) comprises at least one flexible flap (7), particularly a flap forming a lateral part of the wall section. 8. Mandrel according to claim 7, wherein said flexible flap (7) is manufactured of a material that has a lower elastic modulus than an elastic modulus of a remaining part of the wall section. 9. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, including a central support structure (1), wherein radially adjustable wall sections (4-6) defining the outer surface (S) are mechanically coupled (2) to the support structure (1). 10. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, including telescopically coupling means (2), e.g. adjustable spokes (12), which mechanically couple said radially adjustable wall sections (4-6) defining the outer surface (S) to the support structure (1), said coupling means for example comprising at least one crosspiece (13) to adjust the diameter (D). 11. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the mandrel includes at least one driving unit to adjust the diameter (D) of the mandrel. 12. Mandrel according to claim 11, wherein said driving unit to adjust the diameter (D) of the mandrel encompasses a continuously adjustable part. 13. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said first diameter (D l) is in the range of about 3-10 m, wherein said second diameter (D2) is in the range of 4- 15 meter. 14. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a ratio of a maximum achievable diameter to a minimum achievable diameter of the outer surface (S) of the mandrel is in a range of 3: 1 to 1.5: 1. 15. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein during operation a central axis of the mandrel is a substantially vertical axis. 16. Mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the mandrel comprises at least one lifting means (3), for example a hoisting crane or a lifter. 17. Mandrel according to at least claims 9 and 16, wherein the lifting means (3) is connected or connectable to or adjacent to an upper end of the support structure (1). 18. Method for manufacturing substantially cylindrically shaped objects, in particular storage tanks made of plastic, more particularly of fibre or fibre glass reinforced plastic, for example using a mandrel according to any of the preceding claims, wherein an at least one object forming substance (11) is applied onto a substantially cylindrical external surface (S) of a mandrel (M), characterised in that a diameter (D) of the substantially cylindrical external surface is being adjusted before application of the at least one object forming substance (11). 19. Method according to claim 18, wherein at least one driving unit adjusts the diameter (D). 20. Method according to claim 18 or 19, wherein the diameter (D) is adjusted continuously, for example electrically, mechanically and/ or hydraulically. 21. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-20, wherein wall sections (4-6) form the outer surface (S) of the mandrel, wherein the wall sections (4-6) partly overlap each other and slide over each other in a circumferential direction, preferably exerting a radial pressure towards each other. 22. Method according to claim 21, wherein the mandrel reaches a maximal diameter (D) when a circumferential overlap (9) between adjacent wall sections (4-6) reaches a predetermined value, for example of about 10 cm. 23. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-22, wherein a mandrel produces an object with a smooth inner surface. 24. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-23, wherein the mandrel is positioned such that a central line of the mandrel extends in a substantially vertical direction. 25. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-24, wherein the mandrel is equipped with at least one lifting means (3), for example a hoisting crane or a lifter, wherein the lifting means (3) is used to assemble and/or disassemble at least part of the mandrel. 26. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-25, wherein the outer surface(S) of the mandrel is covered with a protective layer, for example a Mylar layer, before applying the at least one object forming substance (11). 27. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-26 wherein the at least one object forming substance (11) includes plastic, preferably a fibre-reinforced polymer. 28. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-27, wherein the mandrel is being rotated around a central axis (1) during and/or after application of the at least one object forming substance (11). 29. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-28, wherein the diameter (D) of the outer surface (S) is decreased, for example by a driving unit, to release a formed object. 30. Method according to any of the preceding claims 18-29, wherein the mandrel is equipped with at least one lifting means (3), for example a hoisting crane or a lifter, wherein the lifting means (3) is used to unload a by the mandrel released substantially cylindrically shaped object. 31. Method according to any of claims 18-30, wherein during a first step the diameter adjustment is carried out manually, the first step setting a desired diameter of a product to be formed, wherein preferably during a second step the diameter adjustment is carried out automatically by at least one drive means, the second step particularly being a step of release of a formed object. 32. Method according to any of the claims 18-31, wherein adjusting a diameter of the mandrel includes repositioning at least two groups of mandrel wall sections (5, 6) at different times, and/or starting such repositioning at different times. 33. Method according to claim 32, wherein the mandrel includes alternatingly positioned first (5) and second wall sections (6) that define said 

 outer surface (S), radially inner sides of the first wall sections (5) facing radially outer sides of the second wall sections (6), wherein the first wall sections (5) are divided into at least two groups of outer wall sections, for example only two groups, wherein the different groups of the first wall sections (5) are repositioned during mutually different repositioning time periods as part of a mandrel diameter adjustment. 34. Method according to claim 33, wherein -viewed in a mandrel's circumferential direction- neighbouring first wall sections (5) partly overlap when the mandrel has been adjusted to a certain mandrel minimum diameter.
